summary: Layer an optional project-root `temper.toml` (adopt + extend/override + severity flips) over the by-kind built-in floor contracts in `check`.

per.section: Decision: the author-declared contract lives in `temper.toml`, layered

new.description: Parse an optional `temper.toml` into the author-declared layer â€” per-kind adoptions, override clauses, and severity flips (`required` âź· `advisory`) â€” and produce the effective per-kind `Contract` by layering it onto the embedded floor. Optional file: absent â‡’ the floor is returned unchanged. Closed-vocabulary: an override clause naming an unknown predicate is a load error, exactly as a bare contract's is.

edit.description: Add `pub mod compose;` (the foundation entry owns the module line).

Expose a minimal seam so `compose` can parse an inline `[[clause]]` array from a `temper.toml` `[kind.<k>]` table into typed `Clause`s, reusing the existing closed-vocabulary parser rather than duplicating it.

`check` discovers an optional `temper.toml` at the project root (the invocation dir), layers it over the embedded `BUILTIN_SKILL_CONTRACT`/`BUILTIN_RULE_CONTRACT` per kind, then runs admissibility + conformance on the *effective* contract; absent `temper.toml` reproduces today's floor-only path verbatim.

tests.asserts: A `temper.toml` flipping a skill clause `required`â†’`advisory` turns a violating skill from blocking to non-blocking; an override that adds a clause makes a previously-clean artifact fire; an inline override naming an unknown predicate is a load error; an inadmissible override (e.g. an empty `enum`) fails admissibility on the effective contract; absent `temper.toml` leaves the floor outcome byte-for-byte unchanged.

acceptance: `check` loads an optional project-root `temper.toml`, layers its per-kind adoptions / overrides / severity-flips over the embedded by-kind floor, runs both greens (admissibility + conformance) on the effective contract, and maps the result to the exit code; with no `temper.toml` every existing test stays green (floor unchanged).
notes: Scope is the committed `temper.toml` adopt/extend-override/severity tier only. Follow-ons (next plan ticks): the role + `verified_by` roster â€” the interface/trait tier that no built-in can carry â€” and the gitignored `temper-local.toml` second layer. `(harness-contract-provisioning)` home/selection half is RESOLVED (this file is its `temper.toml` home), so no fork dependency. Module name `compose` is a suggestion; build owns it.
